Software Comparison

Contractor Foreman vs. JobTread (2026)

Two affordable platforms for contractors who don't want to pay Buildertrend prices. Here's how they differ — and which one wins.

Contractor Foreman and JobTread both target contractors who need more than a spreadsheet but aren't ready for (or don't want to pay for) Buildertrend's $499/month entry point. The two platforms are often compared — both are affordable, both cover project management and job costing — but they have meaningfully different strengths.

Quick comparison

Contractor ForemanJobTread
Starting price$49/mo$99/mo
Free trial30 days14 days
Best forFeature breadth on a budgetJob costing accuracy and financial clarity
Job costingAdequate⭐ Excellent
EstimatingGoodGood
Client portalBasicGood
SchedulingGoodGood
Feature count⭐ Very broadMore focused
UI polishFunctional⭐ More modern

Pricing in detail

Contractor Foreman starts at $49/month and scales based on features, not users. Their plans include:

  • Basic: $49/mo — Core project management, estimates, invoices
  • Standard: $79/mo — Adds scheduling, time tracking, more features
  • Plus: $125/mo — Adds advanced job costing, equipment tracking
  • Unlimited: $166/mo — All features, priority support

JobTread is simpler: one plan starting at $99/month with all features included, scaling by team size for larger organizations. The starting price includes unlimited projects and a clean, complete feature set.

Job costing: JobTread wins clearly

This is the most significant difference between the platforms. JobTread's job costing is real-time and deeply integrated:

  • Every estimate line item flows into a cost bucket
  • Purchase orders create committed costs before invoices arrive
  • Budget vs. actual updates live as you work
  • Margin visibility across all active projects on one dashboard

Contractor Foreman has job costing features, but they're less deeply integrated and require more manual work to keep current. For contractors who've been burned by not knowing their job margin until after the job is done, JobTread's system is a material improvement.

Feature breadth: Contractor Foreman wins

Contractor Foreman packs an impressive number of features into its plans — especially at the budget tiers. You get equipment tracking, fleet management, inspections, checklists, safety tools, lead management, a client portal, HR document storage, and more. For contractors who need a wide array of tools at the lowest possible cost, Contractor Foreman is hard to beat on breadth.

JobTread is more focused — it does fewer things, but what it does (estimates, job costing, project management, client communication) it does better.

Estimating: roughly equal

Both platforms handle estimates competently. You can build line-item estimates, apply markup, generate client-facing proposals, and convert estimates to active projects. Neither has the deep estimating capabilities of UDA ConstructionOnline or dedicated estimating tools, but both are more than adequate for most contractors.

Client experience: JobTread wins slightly

JobTread's client portal is more polished and easier for homeowners to navigate. Contractor Foreman has a client portal, but it's more utilitarian. For contractors who want to impress clients with a professional digital experience, JobTread has the edge — though neither matches Buildertrend's client portal.

Who should choose Contractor Foreman

  • Small contractors who need the widest feature set at the lowest price
  • Operations that need equipment tracking, fleet management, or safety tools
  • Contractors whose primary need is "all the basic tools in one place" at under $100/month
  • Teams where job costing is a nice-to-have rather than a core need

Who should choose JobTread

  • Contractors who've lost money on jobs and want real-time financial visibility
  • Growing businesses that want a platform they won't outgrow quickly
  • Teams that care about UI quality and adoption — staff are more likely to actually use JobTread
  • Contractors who want to eventually scale toward Buildertrend-level capability without a full platform switch

JobTread's job costing is worth the extra $50/month for contractors who track margins seriously.

Try JobTread Free — 14 Days

The verdict

If price is everything: Contractor Foreman at $49/month delivers more features per dollar than almost any competitor. If job costing matters: JobTread at $99/month delivers meaningfully better financial visibility. Most contractors who care about knowing their actual margin will find JobTread's extra cost justified within a month or two.

For contractors who've outgrown both platforms, Buildertrend is the natural next step:

When you're ready to scale up — Buildertrend is the market leader in residential construction software.

Explore Buildertrend
